fields.caption: you can't lose money on every dollar earned / if you spend $2.60 for every $1 you make

narrative_summary: The joke uses the Roll Safe 'big brain' format to reframe OpenAI's catastrophic $2.60-per-$1-earned burn rate as smug self-defeating logic, grounding the punchline in the specific financial ratio from the story.
rationale: Candidate 1 earns its score by weaponizing the Roll Safe format perfectly — the template's smug self-defeating logic is an ideal vessel for the $2.60-per-$1 ratio, and the punchline doesn't just restate the setup but reframes catastrophic failure as a deliberate strategy. The specific financial ratio grounds it in the actual story while the format inversion (the 'smart' move is the dumb move) delivers genuine surprise.
story_title: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, with Spending Hitting $34B
place: in a live coding interview that has already gone off the rails
hat_pair: foam dartboard hat with a plastic dart stuck in the bullseye
giant foam CPU chip worn flat as a hat with heat sink fins poking up
